text:
Admission Date:  1997-02-27       Discharge Date:


Service:

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:  This is a 77-year-old right
handed man with a history of hypertension, two prior
seizures, last in 2000, had atrial fibrillation in sinus
since 02-26.  He was switched from Coumadin to Aspirin in
02.  He was in his usual state of health last night when
he went to bed at around 12 p.m.  He woke up, voice slurred
and recognized that he could not move his left arm and leg.
Denies headache.

MEDICATIONS:  Atenolol, Amiodarone, Hydrochlorothiazide,
Vitamin D, stool softeners.

ALLERGIES:  Percocet, question seizure.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:  Hypertension, paroxysmal atrial
fibrillation, was on Coumadin and Amiodarone but Coumadin was
stopped in 02.  PE in 2000, seizures in 2000.  Surgical
history of partial prostatectomy.

SOCIAL HISTORY:  No tobacco, no alcohol.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:  Neuro, somnolent, arousable with
verbal stimuli, falls back asleep again, follows simple
commands, answers questions appropriately.  Speech is slurred
with pooling secretions; clear with cough.  Speech, tells his
name, hospital, could not tell the date.  Does not answer to
"What happened to you?".  Denies headache.  Cranial nerves,
pupils equal and reactive, extraocular motions mild right
gaze deviation, able to look to the left, mild abduction
defect on the left eye.  Decreased saccades to the left.
Fundus could not be visualized.  Visual fields, no blink to
the left but able to see fingers on both sides.  Decreased
nasolabial fold.  Tongue deviates to the left.  Gags with
cough.  Shoulder shrug to the left.  Motor, no movement on
the left upper or lower extremity, positive triple flexion on
lower extremity, spontaneous command movements on the right,
full strength.  Deep tendon reflexes 0 and 1, no ataxia on
the right, increased reflexes on the right.  MRI in the
Emergency Room showed a large intracerebral hemorrhage
located within the posterior right frontal lobe measuring
approximately 5.8 to 5.7 cm.  Area of hemorrhage demonstrates
irregular contours with hemorrhagic components of multiple
age.  There is a fluid level present within the most
anterolateral portion as well as scattered areas of
susceptibility.  There is extension of the hemorrhage into
the right lateral ventricle with intraventricular hemorrhage
present most dependent portion.  There is some age symmetry
of the lateral ventricles with the right slightly larger than
the left.  There is T2 high signal associated with the
hemorrhage but no significant shift of normal midline
structures.  There are scattered punctate lesions of T2
hyperintensities scattered throughout the white matter of the
centrum semi ovale and the corona radiata.  Abnormal
restrictive diffusion in the area of this hemorrhage.  MRA of
the head, right MCA demonstrates decreased signal compared
with the left side, no aneurysm seen.  Internal carotid
arteries did not appear stenotic.

HOSPITAL COURSE:  He was taken to angiogram to have his bleed
evaluated.  Whether this was a bleed into a stroke or
vascular malformation.  His angiogram was normal showing no
evidence of any vascular malformation and no occlusion of the
MCA even though it was seen on the MRI.  They thought it was
an artifact, thus this was deemed not a hemorrhage into a
stroke but rather amyloid angiopathy.  The patient was
intubated for the angio and then taken to the neuro ICU where
he was observed.  He was also started on Dilantin at this
time.  On 05-10 it was noted that his CK's had come back
positive at 816 and his troponin was 2.3.  His CK and
troponin were followed, continued to rise and peaked at a CPK
Of 816 with troponin of 4.4 and then started trending down.
He was in sinus rhythm and had no EKG changes with this.
Cardiology was consulted, however, and recommended blood
pressure control as his neurologic status tolerated.  In
addition, he did well but after extubation had difficulty
with his secretions and required increased suctioning.  There
was a concern of aspiration pneumonia, had rales bilaterally,
however.  He was started on Levo and Flagyl, however his
chest x-ray did not show any evidence of pneumonia.  The
patient was continued on frequent chest PT with eventual
stabilization of his pulmonary status, decreased necessity
for suctioning.  The patient remained somnolent and with
difficulty waking up, thus speech and swallow was not able to
be performed and PEG tube was placed without complication and
tube feeds were then started.  Because he remained somnolent,
head CT was obtained since he could have hydrocephalus from
blood in the ventricles, no change in ventricular size and he
was transferred out onto the floor on 2019-10-14 where he
continued to do well and EEG was done showing abnormal EEG in
waking state due to widespread slowing of the right
hemisphere, showing focal abnormality likely related to
hemorrhage.  Nevertheless, background remained normal over
the left side, no epileptiform features.  Echo was performed
to evaluate his cardiac status showing mild dilatation of his
left atrium, left ventricular wall thicknesses are normal,
left ventricular cavity mildly dilated.  Aortic valve
leaflets mildly thickened, tricuspid valve leaflets mildly
thickened, no pericardial effusion.  Compared to the echo in
2007, the pulmonary artery systolic pressure is slightly
higher.  The patient continued to do well, was evaluated by
PT, OT and deemed to need rehab.

DISCHARGE DIAGNOSIS:
1.  Right frontal bleed, likely due to amyloid angiopathy.
2.  Non Q wave MI.
3.  Paroxysmal atrial fibrillation.
4.  Likely aspiration pneumonia.

DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:  Amiodarone 400 mg po tid times three
days and 400 mg po bid times a week, then 400 mg q d starting
on 03-19, Captopril 25 mg per PEG tid, titrate for blood
pressure less than 140, Lopressor 100 mg per PEG tid, hold
for systolic blood pressure less than 110, Heparin 5000 units
subcu Brooks Rehabilitation Hospital, Dilantin 100 mg per PEG tube tid, Prevacid 30 mg
po bid per G tube feeds, ProMod with fiber goal of 75 cc,
Levaquin 500 mg per NG tube until 1977-04-28, Flagyl 500 mg po q
8 hours until 1977-04-28.

Discharged to rehab.




                            DR.Edwards,Andrea 13-242

Dictated By:Morris
